tier1.title: Conceptual Foundation
tier1.content: Circular motion involves changing direction of velocity, which means acceleration toward the center even at constant speed. What keeps the object moving in a circle? Is this uniform (constant speed) or non-uniform circular motion?

tier2.title: Relevant Principles
tier2.content: Centripetal acceleration: a_c = v²/r = ω²r, directed toward the center. Centripetal force: F_c = mv²/r = mω²r — this is the net force toward the center, not a separate force. Period: T = 2πr/v = 2π/ω. Universal gravitation: F_g = Gm₁m₂/r².

tier3.title: Targeted Guidance
tier3.content: Identify what force (or component) provides the centripetal acceleration: tension, friction, normal, gravity, or a combination. Draw the FBD and find the net inward force. For orbits, gravity provides F_c: GmM/r² = mv²/r.

tier4.title: Near-Solution Scaffold
tier4.content: Set ΣF_inward = mv²/r. Substitute known values. For banked curves: N sin θ may contribute to centripetal force. For vertical circles: weight components change with position — analyze top and bottom separately. Check that your centripetal force is truly net inward force.

tier5.title: Full Solution Placeholder
tier5.content: Tier 5 is generated by the AI tutor with a complete solution and reasoning comparison.

tier1.title: Conceptual Foundation
tier1.content: In uniform circular motion, speed is constant but velocity changes direction. What is the radius of the path? What force constrains the object to the circle?

tier2.title: Relevant Principles
tier2.content: Velocity is tangent to the circle; acceleration points to the center. Frequency f = 1/T. Angular velocity ω = 2πf = v/r. The centripetal force requirement is always F_net,inward = mv²/r.

tier3.title: Targeted Guidance
tier3.content: Don't add a 'centripetal force' to your FBD — identify the real forces. For a car on a flat turn, friction provides F_c. On a string, tension provides F_c. At minimum speed for vertical loop top, tension can be zero: mg = mv²/r.

tier4.title: Near-Solution Scaffold
tier4.content: Write the specific force equation: e.g., f = mv²/r, T − mg = mv²/r (bottom of loop), T + mg = mv²/r (top of loop). Solve for the unknown. Convert between v, ω, T, and f as needed using v = 2πr/T.

tier5.title: Full Solution Placeholder
tier5.content: Tier 5 is generated by the AI tutor with a complete solution and reasoning comparison.
